headline: Ford Recalls Over 4 Million Vehicles And Tests Investor Confidence
summary: Ford Motor (NYSE:F) has initiated major safety recalls covering more than 4,000,000 trucks and SUVs in the U.S. The recalls address software errors that can affect braking and exterior lighting performance. Additional actions target faults in rear suspension components that may impair steering control. The fixes span both software updates and hardware repairs across a range of recent Ford models. For investors watching NYSE:F at a current share price of $14.09, these recalls occur during a...

headline: Dow Jones leads modest retreat as Wall Street aloof to Iran war

headline: Carl Icahn and Other Insiders Are Doubling Down Amid Market Volatilities
summary: Insider buying can signal confidence when executives and major shareholders put their own money on the line. While not foolproof, these transactions offer a window into how those closest to a company view its prospects. Recent filings show notable insider activity across several stocks, spanning a turnaround car rental giant to the world’s largest alternative ... headline: The Obscure Fee Costing You More to Buy a Car
summary: It is called the “destination charge” and is now running an average $1,600.  Automakers have been upping the fee by hundreds of dollars in recent years, as fuel and shipping costs have risen.  Overall, car buyers spent more than $26 billion to cover destination charges in 2025, according to data from Edmunds, the online car-shopping resource.
